title: Add MineflayerAdapter auto-reconnect logic with exponential backoff
description:
**Source:** External audit | **Council:** P1

**Problem:** `bot.on('kicked', ...)` and `bot.on('error', ...)` at index.js:317-318 only log events and send them to C#. There is no `bot.on('end', ...)` handler and no retry logic. When the bot is kicked (server restart, AFK timeout, network hiccup), the process is permanently stuck pointing at a dead `bot` object. Every subsequent dispatched action throws a raw TypeError, which `classifyError()` cannot map to `reasonCode: 'not_connected'` because the error text doesn't contain `'not spawned'` or `'not connected'`.

**Fix:** Add a `bot.on('end', ...)` handler that:
1. Logs the disconnection at `warn` level
2. Sends a `disconnected` event to C# side
3. Reconnects using exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 60s)
4. Re-registers all event handlers after reconnect
5. On successful reconnect, sends a `reconnected` event

**Note:** TSK-0066 covers the C# side reconnect handling and is already Done. This task covers the JS side which currently has none.

**Files:** `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` (add handler near line 317)

**Validation:** Kill MC server while bot is connected — verify adapter logs reconnect attempts. Restart server — verify bot reconnects and resumes.
